**Key Responsibilities:**
* Allocate labour and skills effectively to optimise throughput and minimise bottlenecks.
* Support short- to medium-term capacity planning and resource forecasting.
* Track and report KPIs including delivery performance, quality metrics, productivity, and safety.
* Drive workflow improvements to reduce waste and improve efficiency.
* Support new product introduction (NPI) and low-volume or prototype builds where required.
**Front-Line Leadership & Team Management:**
* Provide day-to-day leadership, direction, and technical support to Cell Leaders and the production team, with direct line responsibility for the Cell Leads.
* Lead by example with visible shop-floor presence and high standards.
* Create a high-performance, accountable, and inclusive team culture.
* Conduct regular one-to-ones, performance reviews, and development discussions.
* Coach, mentor, and upskill cell leaders and facilitate training of technical staff.
* Monitor workload distribution and ensure clear accountability for deliverables.
* Recognise achievement and address underperformance promptly and fairly.
* Encourage open communication, collaboration, and continuous feedback.
**Operational & Technical Oversight:**
* Oversee assembly, integration, wiring, and testing activities in line with engineering drawings and work instructions.
* Ensure strict adherence to aerospace standards and documented processes.
* Support detailed manufacturing activities where required, including fault diagnosis and problem resolution.
* Ensure accurate completion of production and test documentation for full traceability.
* Maintain high standards of housekeeping, 6S, and FOD control.
* Ensure compliance within ESD-controlled and regulated manufacturing environments.
**Quality Assurance & Regulatory Compliance:**
* Ensure full compliance with AS9100, ISO, FAA/EASA (as applicable), and internal quality systems.
* Promote a right-first-time and zero-defect culture.
* Partner with Quality to manage non-conformances and corrective actions.
* Lead root cause investigations and implement sustainable corrective actions.
* Maintain audit readiness and support internal and external audits.
* Health, Safety & Environmental Leadership
* Promote and maintain a safe and compliant working environment.
* Ensure adherence to Health & Safety, COSHH, Human Factors, Tool Control, and environmental standards.
* Conduct regular safety reviews and proactively address risks.
* Champion continuous improvement initiatives relating to safety and operational standards.
**Reporting & Cross-Functional Collaboration:**
* Provide regular performance updates to Production Management.
* Communicate risks, constraints, and mitigation plans clearly and proactively.
* Liaise with Engineering, Supply Chain, and Quality to ensure material availability, technical clarity, and issue resolution.
* Use production data and metrics to drive informed decision-making.
* Proven experience in a manufacturing or production leadership role within aerospace or a highly regulated industry.
* Strong hands-on background in assembly, integration, or testing within a precision environment.
* Demonstrated experience delivering against production schedules and OTIF targets.
* Experience leading or mentoring technical teams.
* Working knowledge of aerospace quality systems (AS9100, ISO standards).
* Strong understanding of ESD-controlled environments and compliance standards.
* Excellent problem-solving and root cause analysis capability.
* Ability to work to strict deadlines in a fast-paced environment.
* Strong communication and interpersonal skills.
* Good IT literacy including ERP/MRP systems and Microsoft Office.
* Desirable
**Skills & Experience:**
* Lean Manufacturing or Six Sigma knowledge.
* Experience in cell-based or pulse-line production systems.
* IOSH / NEBOSH qualification.
* Experience in low-volume, high-complexity or prototype manufacturing.
* Tool control management experience.
* Formal supervisory or leadership training.
**Qualifications:**
* Aerospace or Engineering Full Apprenticeship.
* NVQ Level 3 in Aerospace Engineering or equivalent technical discipline.
* Additional leadership or continuous improvement certifications desirable.
* The environment includes exposure to machinery, noise, and regulated manufacturing conditions. Occasional travel may be required for audits, supplier visits, or training.

# Privacy policy for the AALTO employer branding and recruitment
Date of publication: 07-06-2024
We at AALTO manage our employer branding and recruitment process through our (the “Career Site”), and by using a related applicant tracking system.
In this privacy policy, we explain how we process your personal data if:
This privacy policy also describes what rights you have when we process your personal data, and how you can exercise these rights.
When we use the term “Candidate” in this privacy policy, we are referring to each of Connecting Candidates; Applying Candidates; Sourced Candidates; and Referred Candidates, unless it’s stated otherwise.
### 1. About processing of personal data
Personal data is all information that can be directly or indirectly linked to a living, physical person. Examples of personal data are: name, e-mail address, telephone number and IP address. Processing of personal data is any automated use of personal data - such as collecting, creating, analyzing, sharing, and deleting personal data.
There are laws and regulations on how companies may process personal data, so-called data protection laws. Different data protection laws apply to different types of use of personal data, and in different parts of the world. An example of a data protection law that is relevant for our use of your personal data, as described in this privacy policy, is the EU Data Protection Regulation (2016/679, “GDPR”).
Most obligations under the GDPR apply to the so-called data controller. A data controller is the entity that decides for which purposes personal data will be processed, and how the processing will be executed. The data controller can use a so-called data processor. A data processor is an entity that is only allowed to process personal data as instructed by the data controller, and may not use the personal data for its own purposes.
We are the data controller when we process your personal data as described in this Privacy policy.

##### Candidates
##### All individuals

Welcome to AALTO, a global leader in Stratospace. Based in Farnborough, AALTO is an Airbus subsidiary that manufactures, operates, and delivers services with its solar-powered, stratospheric platform: Zephyr.
Zephyr’s persistence enables continuous flight for months at a time. Its current flight-time record of over 67 days in the stratosphere. From above 60,000+ft, or 20km above ground, AALTO can deliver several applications including direct-to-device connectivity and high-resolution Earth observation.
AALTO operates in a new operational layer between most of air traffic and low-Earth orbit, Stratospace. This is where high-altitude platforms led by Zephyr deliver services to commercial and government partners. We can help monitor environments, observe borders, track natural disasters and connect unconnected communities.
Join us as we create a new domain of aerospace: Stratospace. You’ll be part of a highly-skilled, innovative and passionate group of people – Team AALTO. Together, we can Unlock Stratospace.
**Job Purpose:**
The Senior HR Business Partner plays a pivotal role in shaping and delivering AALTO’s people strategy, combining hands‐on partnership with high‐impact, employee‐focused, and commercially aligned HR support. This role requires deep HR expertise, strong relationship‐building capability, and a proactive, solutions‐oriented mindset to drive organisational performance, foster a high‐trust leadership culture, and enable the ongoing evolution of the business.
As a trusted advisor to leaders across the organisation (primarily in the UK and Kenya), you will champion change, strengthen people processes, enhance employee engagement, and support capability and workforce development. You will balance strategic influence with operational excellence - navigating ambiguity with confidence and driving initiatives that directly contribute to AALTO’s long‐term success.
**Key Responsibilities:**
* Collaborate with senior leaders and managers to shape, influence, and deliver the overarching people strategy.
* Use data, insights, and workforce metrics to identify people priorities, challenges, and drive evidence‐based decision‐making.
* Develop HR solutions that support the business in achieving strategic and operational goals.
* Lead and support organisation‐wide change initiatives, ensuring alignment with business needs and effective communication across teams.
* Act as a trusted advisor to leaders on all HR matters, including organisational design, employee relations, performance, and talent development.
* Foster a strong leadership and coaching culture across the organisation.
* Build influential relationships across all levels, enhancing collaboration and strengthening HR’s role as a strategic partner.
* Oversee and optimise the utilisation of HiBob, ensuring high levels of data integrity and meaningful reporting.
* Maintain and enhance HR processes, policies, and service delivery, ensuring efficiency, scalability, and an exceptional employee experience.
* Drive continuous improvement by evaluating existing practices and implementing enhancements aligned with business goals.
* Lead the continuous embedding of performance management and goal‐setting frameworks.
* Provide expert guidance on employee relations issues, including grievances, performance concerns, and policy interpretation.
* Monitor, review, and update HR policies to ensure compliance with employment legislation and best practice.
* Lead initiatives to enhance employee engagement, including survey design, delivery, and action planning.
* Help cultivate a positive, inclusive, and high‐performance culture across the organisation.
* Manage relationships with compensation and benefits suppliers, brokers, and external partners, ensuring value for money, compliance, and high‐quality service delivery aligned to the People & Culture strategy.
**Qualifications & Experience:**
* Progressive HR Business Partnering experience, with proven impact in strategic and fast‐paced environments.
* Bachelor’s degree in HR, or CIPD Level 7, or equivalent experience, required.
* Strong understanding of HR operations, employee experience, employment law, and best‐practice HR methodologies.
* Experience in an Engineering, Technology, and/or Manufacturing environment preferred.
* Demonstrated success in leading change management and employee communication initiatives across a geographically dispersed workforce.
* Experience with HRIS platforms, ideally HiBob.
* Strong critical thinking and problem‐solving skills, with the ability to navigate complex issues and provide pragmatic solutions.
* Proven ability to thrive in a dynamic, ambiguous environment with resilience and composure.
* Highly proactive, relationship‐driven, and committed to continuous personal and professional development.
* Interest in Compensation & Benefits preferred.
* Hybrid role: 3 days per week in the Farnborough office.
* Occasional global travel may be required.

## **JOB DESCRIPTION:**
Abbott’s Electrophysiology (EP) division is at the forefront of transforming cardiac care through cutting-edge technologies that diagnose and treat abnormal heart rhythms. With a fast-growing portfolio that includes advanced mapping systems, ablation catheters, and remote monitoring solutions, Abbott empowers clinicians to restore healthy heart function and improve patient outcomes.
We are recruiting for an experienced medical devices leader to join us as **General Manager EP, UK & Ireland**, responsible for developing strategic objectives for the region and translating into operating plans to grow sales and profit in line with agreed targets. The General Manger will build and lead a high performance organization to support delivery, ensuring operational integrity and compliance throughout.

Education

## **About this role**
Ably is a product-led growth business. Our platform provides developers with the infrastructure to build, extend, and deliver powerful realtime experiences, and our self-service segment is a critical part of that PLG motion. We believe that great developer experience; smooth onboarding, excellent documentation, and the right touchpoints at the right moments, is what delivers growth.
We're looking for a Developer Growth Manager to own that experience for our self-service segment. You'll sit within the Developer Experience team and take responsibility for the developer journey: from sign-up through activation, adoption, and expansion. Your primary tools will be data, analytics, and automation, but you'll also engage directly with developers to understand how they move through the funnel. All with the intention of providing developers with an industry-leading developer experience.
This is an AI-forward role. You'll be using AI to accelerate everything you do, whether that’s running experiments at pace, implementing changes, conducting research, capturing insights, you’ll be moving faster than would be possible through manual effort alone.
## **Day to day you will**
* **Use AI to produce quality content.** You’ll write reusable prompts and skills that docs contributors can use to ensure their content meets Ably’s quality standards.
* **Understand how developers experience Ably.** You'll empathise with how developers work, understand their workflows, and use that understanding to identify friction, suggest improvements to onboarding flows, and remove barriers to adoption. A key part of this role is being able to think like a developer and credibly engage with a technical audience.
* **Use data to drive decisions.** Data and monitoring are your primary tools and you'll use them to identify at-risk accounts, product-qualified leads, and expansion opportunities across the self-service segment.
* **Design and run automated engagement programs.** You'll help design onboarding sequences, in-app messaging, and email nurture campaigns that guide developers from sign-up to first value and beyond. You'll continuously experiment with these flows using AI to iterate at pace.
* **Drive activation, adoption, and retention.**
* **Improve the self-service experience end to end.** You'll identify friction points in the product, docs, and onboarding flows, and work directly with other teams to address them. You'll advocate for changes that make Ably delightful for developers to use and succeed with on their own.
* **Feed insights back to the business.** You'll share usage data, trends, customer feedback, and insights from customer insights and calls to influence roadmap decisions and go-to-market strategy.
* **Support a product-led sales motion.** You'll design automated journeys triggered by usage signals, identify expansion opportunities, and hand qualified leads to the sales team when accounts show signs of outgrowing self-service.**Use data to drive decisions.** Data and monitoring are your primary tools and you'll use them to identify at-risk accounts, product-qualified leads, and expansion opportunities across the self-service segment.
## **What success looks like**
* Reduced time to value for developers signing up to Ably.
* Drove revenue growth through the self-service segment.
* Improved conversion rates across the sign-up to activation funnel.
* Identified and surfaced expansion opportunities to the sales team.
* Assisted the wider Developer Experience team to improve the onboarding experience and made the product more delightful for developers.
* Used AI and data to move fast, run experiments, and continuously iterated on the developer journey.
## **We'd love to talk if you have**
* A technical background so that you can understand and credibly engage with developers.
* Experience in a product-led growth, DevRel or technical marketing role; ideally within a self-serve or developer-focused SaaS business.
* A strong analytical mindset, with hands-on experience using product usage data and tools to understand and act on customer behaviour.
* Experience of using AI as part of your workflow and an understanding of how it can accelerate your research, experimentation and analysis. Ultimately how it can assist you to iterate and ship faster.
* A genuine empathy for developers and technical users, and an understanding of what great developer experience looks like.
* A proactive, self-directed approach.
* Strong cross-functional collaboration skills, and the confidence to engage and speak directly with developers.
## **Bonus points if you have**
* Familiarity with product analytics tools.
* A proven ability to build and optimize automated engagement programs across email, in-app, and other digital channels.
* Experience working with APIs or developer tools, or a background that gives you genuine understanding of a technical customer base.
* Experience defining and tracking PLG metrics such as activation rate, time to value, and PQL conversion.

job_description:

**Who we are:**
**Access Partnership makes innovative technology work for the world.**
Access Partnership makes innovation work for the world, guiding businesses and governments through complex regulatory challenges. It shapes regulations and policy that are fair and which enable market access for innovative companies, drive growth, and attract investment into national markets. The firm’s roster of world-leading clients includes the largest tech and innovation companies, major government bodies, and multilateral lenders and development organisations.
The Space & Connectivity Practice engages in technical intergovernmental and industry fora on a global level, representing client interests and effecting change in satellite and telecom regulation. We perform radio compatibility studies, regulatory analyses and network coordination, in support of planned and operational networks. We also effect change in regulation and standards, to reflect novel approaches and ensure network innovation is not constrained by outdated rules.
We are seeking recent graduates or professionals with 2–3 years of experience in roles such as Spectrum Engineer, RF Engineer, Radio Frequency Engineer, Regulatory Engineer, RF Compatibility Engineer, Satellite Engineer, or Junior to Senior Engineering positions.
**What you'll do:**
* Prepare ITU satellite filings for new and amended GSO satellite networks
* Prepare ITU satellite filings for new and amended non-GSO satellite systems
* Monitor and review ITU/BR weekly circulars (“IFICs”), and prepare responses to be sent through the notifying administration
* Carry out compatibility studies between satellite networks (GSO/GSO; GSO/non-GSO, non-GSO/non-GSO), analyse results and prepare reports case by case
* Carry out sharing and compatibility studies between services within existing allocations and potential new services considered by WRCs agenda items of interest to the clients
* Develop coordination proposals within the framework of the ITU Radio Regulations, and participate in negotiations with operators/administrations to reach coordination agreements
* Prepare draft coordination agreements under the ITU framework, considering the results of the compatibility studies conducted through Visualyse software and other tools as well as the relevant ITU RR provisions.
* Liaise with clients and team members to report progress and review priorities
* Research technical regulations and standards, and develop strategies for effecting change through national, regional, and global fora
* Undertake competitive intelligence gathering on the satellite industry.
**What you'll need:**
*Essential*
* Quantitative background
* Deep understanding of radio spectrum, and its uses by satellite and other radio systems
* Deep understanding of the ITU Radio Regulations
* A demonstrable interest in the technology industry
* Excellent written and oral English language skills
*Desirable:*
* Fluency in a second language
* Advanced in Visualyse software
* Basic to Intermediate in Sat-Coord Software
* Proficiency in MATLAB software
**What We Offer:**
Our diverse international client base of technology giants and start-ups will provide the opportunity to work on multiple international projects on behalf of some of the industry’s leading companies. Our firm boasts a collaborative working environment, social events and a robust support system. We are committed to providing training and professional development for all colleagues.
Access Partnerships seeks an intellectually curious candidate with proven quantitative capabilities, excellent communication and analytical skills, and an ability to formulate and present complex technical information in a clear and concise manner. The selected candidate will have a strong interest in radio communications and satellite engineering, and will be responsible for managing relationships with some of the largest ICT companies in the world. **Space & Connectivity Practice**

# Senior Control Systems Engineer
**Senior Control Systems Engineer (Control Systems Integration)**
**Job Description**
As a Senior Control Systems Engineer, you will work closely with our Senior Engineering Delivery Manager, you will be responsible for the full life cycle delivery of the project in both technical compliance and quality, from design and development through to programming, testing, and commissioning.
As a Senior Control Systems Engineer, you will bring an innovative approach to problem solving, you will be working with highly skilled engineers who you will be responsible for managing and motivating to get the best delivery output. This includes mentoring our graduates and apprentices.
Our ideal candidate will have the knowledge and experience to help the office and team grow.
The ideal candidate will have previous experience in a similar environment and good knowledge of PLC’s, SCADA, HMI, variable speed drives and instrumentation products.
**Full-time position** **(08:30-17:15 Monday to Thursday and 08:30-16:00 on Fridays)**
This is a hands-on senior engineering role where you will:
* Work closely with clients to develop solutions and ensure high-quality project delivery.
* Lead and mentor our highly skilled engineers, including graduates and apprentices, to help the team perform at its best.
* Design and implement advanced control systems using PLC, SCADA, and HMI technologies.
* Bring a problem-solving mindset to complex engineering challenges and drive technical excellence.
**Key Responsibilities**
* Lead the design, development, and commissioning of control systems
* Attend client meetings, conduct site surveys, and provide technical leadership
* Programme and configure PLC and HMI/SCADA software (Siemens, Rockwell, Mitsubishi, Wonderware/ AVEVA, ClearScada/ GEOSCADA etc.)
* Produce detailed project documentation and ensure technical compliance
* Oversee site commissioning (occasional short periods away from home)
**What we require from you:**
* 5+ years’ experience in a Senior or Lead Control Systems Engineering role,
* Degree in Software or Electrical Engineering, or equivalent qualifications
* Mentoring or team leadership experience
* Proven ability to design and modify PLC and SCADA/HMI software
* Experience with Allen Bradley, Siemens, Mitsubishi, or Wonderware systems (other platforms also considered)
* Experience in producing detailed documentation, and client communication
* Instrumentation and Electrical experience would be preferable
* Full UK driving licence and eligibility to work in the UK
**What you will get in return**
* Auto enrolment pension scheme (including salary sacrifice/exchange for pension payments if desired)
* Private health scheme (after 1 year service)
* Death in service plan (after 5 years service)
* 25 days holiday plus bank holidays (raises to 30 days after 5 years of service)
* Company car scheme
* Access to our Wellbeing programme operated through COGG ()
* Access to 24/7 365 days confidential employee counselling service, free for employees and their families
* Access to our Training Hub (various training courses available)
* Annual Personal development and salary reviews
* Free parking at all our offices (Subject to availability & any company parking policies or parking rotas that may be in effect)
* Access to company vans or pool cars (company car scheme also available)
* Access to Perkbox (https://join.perkbox.co.uk) This gives employees access to discounted shopping, eating out, cinema and even holidays
* Free quarterly social events, team building days and Christmas party weekend break.
* TIL / Overtime and expenses scheme
**Our company**
At Adsyst Automation Ltd we understand that our continued success is down to our dedicated team, therefore, we invest time and effort in each individual; training and assisting with their personal and professional development, encouraging their strengths while offering a good work/ life balance.
Adsyst Automation Ltd has been providing complex Control Systems for Process Control, Automation and Telemetry to customers worldwide for over 35 years. We have four offices nationally; our Head office in Yateley, Hampshire, our Stafford office and a further two offices in Peterborough and Greater London.
With our core skills lying in PLC, SCADA, HMI, MIS, MES, RTU, control system software engineering, data acquisition, bespoke software and LV Assemblies and Electrical installation, our expertise in panel building and electrical installation allows us to offer full turnkey solutions.
We approach each project with a fresh outlook and use our knowledge and partnerships to stay a step ahead of the competition, offering the most advanced, innovative and cost-effective solutions.
As we continue to expand and attract top talent, we remain dedicated to maintaining a supportive and inclusive work environment. Following several successful graduate programmes, AIS is looking to employ four graduate engineers, with a focus on design engineering or material science, to join our graduate programme, commencing in October 2026.

The two year scheme will consist of two consecutive one-year placements. The first year will be based in Gloucester and the second year based in Skelmersdale. You will then be invited to apply for a permanent position at the end of the programme. You can expect to receive in-depth, practical experience and training opportunities during your placement.
You will have the opportunity to work with experienced engineers who are playing a leading role in providing products and solutions to the challenging subsea, marine and renewables industries. You will work with the team and contribute to the delivery of high value contracts. You will also gain experience of working within two separate businesses within the AIS group, providing a valuable insight in to how different businesses operate in order to provide best value to their customers.
**The ideal Graduate Materials Engineer will be;**
* Educated to a Degree level in Engineering (Chemical, Mechanical, Naval, or Material Science)
* Strong in maths and physics and able to apply these on a complex concept
* Able to undertake hand calculations, analyse and interpret data, and author technical reports
* Have an understanding of polymers, chemistry, and materials science
* Capable and willing to undertake hands-on, practical activities
* Confident, assertive, and proactive in approach to work, with a ‘can do’ flexible attitude
* Able to work independently on set tasks and schedule workload
* Able to demonstrate high attention to detail
* Proficient in clear, written and verbal communication skills in English
* Innovative and have an agile approach to work
**What are the benefits of being a Graduate Materials Engineer:**
* Salary up to £29,000 per annum
* 25 days holiday per year plus bank holidays
* Automatic enrolment into group personal pension scheme
* Life assurance of 4 x annual salary
* Comprehensive employee assistance programme including 24/7 GP, unlimited mental heath support and legal consultations
* Access to leisure benefits including a cycle to work scheme

**Assessment centre:** Wednesday 29 - Thursday 30 April 2026
**Start date of programme:** 01 October 2026

## Job Description
**WHAT YOU DO AT AMD CHANGES EVERYTHING** At AMD, our mission is to build great products that accelerate next-generation computing experiences—from AI and data centers, to PCs, gaming and embedded systems. Grounded in a culture of innovation and collaboration, we believe real progress comes from bold ideas, human ingenuity and a shared passion to create something extraordinary. When you join AMD, you’ll discover the real differentiator is our culture. We push the limits of innovation to solve the world’s most important challenges—striving for execution excellence, while being direct, humble, collaborative, and inclusive of diverse perspectives. Join us as we shape the future of AI and beyond. **Together, we advance your career.** **Job Summary**
As part of AMD’s distinguished HR Talent Acquisition Team, this leadership role is pivotal within the Workforce Solutions team, driving operational excellence, ensuring compliance, and delivering exemplary service through effective collaboration with internal teams and MSP/VMS partners. The ideal candidate will optimize workforce processes, oversee quality assurance initiatives, and lead strategic improvements.
**Key Responsibilities**
**Operational Coordination & Scheduling**
* Direct day-to-day MSP partner activities, including contractor onboarding oversight, scheduling, and task assignments.
* Work with various teams to proactively prioritize and resolve technical or operational challenges to meet established schedules, SLAs, and KPIs.
* Oversee real-time MSP workflows within MSP Operations, such as ticket queue management.
* Collaborate closely with AMD stakeholders (e.g., Engagement Managers, Operations Teams, HRBPs).
* Serve as a trusted advisor, cultivating a culture of talent advisory within the AMD Workforce Solutions team.
**SLA & Performance Monitoring**
* Partner with MSP representatives to report on SLAs, KPIs, and performance metrics via regular updates.
* Conduct audits in the Vendor Management System (VMS) to ensure compliance with AMD and program standards.
**Supplier & Vendor Management**
* Support initiatives around rate governance, cost controls, and vendor rationalization.
* Attend regular supplier performance reviews, utilize scorecard assessments, and participate in business reviews.
**Governance & Compliance**
* Ensure adherence to labor regulations, safety standards, and classification criteria (including independent contractor review support with EOR/AOR suppliers).
* Maintain accurate documentation, audit preparedness, and compliance with site- and corporate-level policies.
* Facilitate process and policy education for engagement managers and suppliers.
**VMS Technology Management**
* Oversee daily usage of VMS systems, ensuring data integrity, user accuracy, and proper workflow adherence.
* Train hiring managers and suppliers in system processes and updates.
* Collaborate with MSP and internal IT to resolve issues and enhance system utilization.
**Quality Assurance & Coaching**
* Manage QA processes to evaluate MSP deliverables, providing focused feedback and coaching.
* Maintain up-to-date process documentation that accurately reflects current practices.
**Communication & Escalations**
* Act as the primary escalation point, identifying root causes and recommending effective resolutions.
* Ensure consistent communication with stakeholders, enhancing cross-functional collaboration.
**Process Improvement & Strategic Oversight**
* Identify and recommend workflow optimizations and process enhancements proactively.
* Support initiatives to scale and advance the reach, efficiency, and quality of the MSP program.
**Reporting & Documentation**
* Maintain precise records regarding requisition activity, candidate disposition, and onboarding procedures.
* Provide timely and insightful reporting to senior leadership.
**SOW & International Program Support (Preferred)**
* Review SOW requests to determine appropriate worker type, coordinate with suppliers, maintain documentation, and track key milestones (preferred).
* Provide support to international teams/sites as needed, ensuring alignment on global reporting, compliance, and supplier relations.
**Qualifications & Skills**
* Education: Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related discipline (preferred).
* Experience: Minimum five years in MSP coordination or relevant staffing/workforce management roles.
* Proven proficiency in rate card analysis, cost modeling, and vendor negotiations.
* At least two years of leadership experience overseeing direct reports.
* Technical competency with VMS platforms (e.g., Beeline, Fieldglass) and analytical/reporting tools such as Excel or Power BI.
* Strong analytical skills with demonstrated attention to detail.
* Superior verbal and written communication abilities; adept at engaging stakeholders across all levels.
* Demonstrated capacity to manage multiple priorities under tight deadlines within dynamic settings.
* Customer-focused approach with verifiable service delivery expertise.
* Self-driven and proactive, capable of working independently.
